svg.title: NCCL collective execution mental model
svg.desc: Top: a five-stage pipeline from framework call to CUDA kernel, with the planner expanding into algorithms and protocols, the API expanding into eight collectives, and the kernel expanding into N parallel channel rings. Bottom: Ring AllReduce shown as ReduceScatter followed by AllGather across four GPUs.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How does PyTorch call into NCCL?')
sendPrompt('What collectives does the NCCL API expose?')
sendPrompt('How does the NCCL planner pick algorithm and protocol?')
sendPrompt('What does the NCCL CUDA kernel do?')
sendPrompt('What are the 8 NCCL collective primitives?')
sendPrompt('Tell me about NCCL Ring vs Tree vs NVLS algorithms')
sendPrompt('Tell me about NCCL Simple, LL, and LL128 protocols')

svg.text: recv + reduce + send around the ring
recv + copy + send; same ring, no reduce
Each channel runs an independent pipeline; communication and reduction are fused in one CUDA kernel.
